description: NZ curriculum-aligned HTML5 canvas math game for ages 6-12. Ship dodges/catches falling meteors labelled with math answers. Correct answer recharges shield; wrong depletes it.

gameLoop.summary: 3-lane canvas game. One question shown at top. 3 meteors fall (one per lane) labelled with answer choices. Player taps/swipes a lane to move ship. Ship collides with the meteor in its lane when meteors reach ship level — correct = shield/score up, wrong = shield down.

screenFlow: landing  → age-select → mode-select → game → (gameover | results) → landing
Phase 2: landing → (missions | shop | cosmetics) accessible from landing

phases.title: Core Playable Game
features: HTML shell: landing, age-select, mode-select, game-canvas, gameover, results screens
Canvas game engine: requestAnimationFrame loop, scaling, touch/keyboard input
3-lane ship movement with smooth lerp animation
Meteor wave spawning and falling with configurable speed
Collision detection: meteor reaches ship Y → evaluate ship lane
Question types: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, algebra (missing number), number patterns, geometry (perimeter/area), fractions of amounts
Distractor generation: near-miss, common misconceptions, unique guarantees
Shield system: drain per second, recharge on correct, penalty on wrong
Scoring: score, XP, coins with combo multiplier
Adaptive difficulty: adjusts up/down based on last-10-answer accuracy
Game modes: Quick Run (2.5 min, hasFailure), Zen (no drain, no fail)
Age bands: age6_7 (diff 1-3), age8_9 (diff 2-5), age10_12 (diff 3-7) with strand weights
Fever rainbow border + golden glow overlay
LocalStorage persistence: best score, total correct answered
Responsive canvas scaling (mobile portrait and desktop)
knownLimitations: Question types: money, time, statistics, probability not yet included (Phase 2)
No teaching tips / hint overlay after wrong answer (Phase 3)
Ship always rendered as default scout color (Phase 2)

phases.title: Missions, Upgrades & Cosmetics
plannedFeatures: Mission select screen: load from data/missions.json, show progress, goal tracking
Mission mode gameplay: goal-specific win condition (streak, correct count, fever, etc.)
Upgrades screen: purchase with coins, 3 categories (survival, speed_score, math_helper), apply effects in-game
Cosmetics screen: ships (colors), trails, pets — unlock based on conditions
Apply upgrade effects: shield_max, shield_recharge, drain_multiplier, combo_decay_multiplier, xp_multiplier, bonus_coins, tap_cooldown, eliminate_one, slow_on_streak, place_value_highlight
Per-question timing bonus XP (speedBonusXP from config)
Question types: money (NZD), time (clock arithmetic), statistics (read graph values)
Teaching tip card shown after wrong answer: loads from data/teaching.json
Persistent game progress: level, coins, upgrades, cosmetics, missions in localStorage
XP level-up animation in HUD

phases.title: Audio, Polish & Advanced Features
plannedFeatures: Web Audio API: procedural SFX for correct (chime), wrong (thud), fever (swell), level-up (fanfare), game-over (descend)
Background ambient space music (oscillator-based procedural)
Enhanced particle system: trail particles behind ship, explosion rings, star burst on fever
Probability question type (fraction answers)
Number pattern advanced: square numbers, doubling sequences
Statistics: mean/mode questions with simple inline bar chart
Daily challenge mode with fixed seed
Stats screen: accuracy by strand, total XP, level progress bar
Improved meteor visuals: crater texture, glow pulse based on closeness to ship

phases.title: Full Content & NZ Curriculum Coverage
plannedFeatures: All question types from data/questions.json fully implemented
Place value highlight upgrade visual effect
Eliminate-one upgrade: dims a wrong option on meteor
Second chance upgrade: shield protection visual
Timetable/schedule question type
Composite area (L-shape) geometry questions
Fraction equivalence questions
Two-step algebra (balance equations)
Compound probability
Share game result (score card image)
Accessibility: high contrast mode, reduced motion
i18n hook for Te Reo Māori labels (ka rawe, tino pai)

dataFiles.data/config.json: Master config: canvas size, shield values, scoring, combo, adaptive, modes, ageBands, audio, particles
dataFiles.data/questions.json: Question type specs: rangeA/B per difficulty, templates, distractor rules for all 10 question types
dataFiles.data/upgrades.json: 11 upgrades across 3 categories with effect specs
dataFiles.data/cosmetics.json: 8 ships, 5 trails, 5 pets, 5 stickers with unlock conditions
dataFiles.data/missions.json: 13 missions with goals, rewards, and unlock ship references
dataFiles.data/teaching.json: Teaching tips per question type with strategy, visual type, step-by-step examples; correctFeedback strings in Te Reo Māori and English

techStack.runtime: Pure HTML5 + Vanilla JS + Canvas 2D — no build tools, no frameworks
techStack.dataLoading: Game config and question rules are inlined in index.html JS constants for offline/file:// compatibility. Data JSON files are source-of-truth reference.
techStack.persistence: localStorage key: mmsr_stats (bestScore, totalCorrect, level, coins, unlockedShips, unlockedTrails, upgrades, completedMissions)

codingNotes.canvasCoords: Origin top-left. SHIP_Y = 700 (fixed). Meteors start at y = -(METEOR_R*2), fall downward.
codingNotes.laneAssignment: lane 0 = left (x 0-160), lane 1 = center (x 160-320), lane 2 = right (x 320-480). laneCenter(n) = n*160 + 80.
codingNotes.collisionModel: Each meteor checked individually. When m.y >= SHIP_Y - SHIP_H/2 - METEOR_R AND m.lane === shipLane → hit.
codingNotes.questionFlow: spawnQuestion() → generateQuestion() → spawnMeteors(). On any hit (correct or wrong) gs.questionAnswered=true. When all meteors exit canvas → spawnQuestion() again.
codingNotes.adaptiveDifficulty: Uses last-10 answer window. Accuracy >0.85 → difficulty++. Accuracy <0.65 → difficulty--. Clamped to ageBand diffMax.
codingNotes.feverMode: Triggered at combo thresholds [5,10,15]. Lasts 8s. Doubles coins, halves shield drain, enables rainbow border.
codingNotes.rendering: drawBackground() → drawLanes() → drawMeteors() → drawShip() → drawParticles() → drawHUD() → drawFeverEffect()
